title: Topic editor component with document tabs and code editor
purpose: Provides a form for creating or editing topics with multiple document types, code editing capabilities, and save/cancel functionality.
module_context: Exports a TopicEditor React component that manages document state, handles user interactions, and provides save/cancel actions with verification options.

steps.content: Return the language hint for the given document type.
Always return 'markdown' regardless of the document type.
Initialize state for documents, active tab, editor name, and saving status.
Track which documents have been modified using a set.
Determine if the component is in create mode based on whether a topic name is provided.
Set up a memoized check for whether any documents have changed since initial load.
Define a callback to handle document content changes and update the modified documents set.
Define a handler for save actions that validates the editor name and calls the save callback.
Define a handler for cancel actions that confirms with the user if there are unsaved changes.
Render the topic editor UI with header, document tabs, code editor, and action buttons.
Update the document content for the active tab.
Mark the active tab as modified.
Validate that the editor name is provided.
Set the appropriate saving state based on whether verification is requested.
Call the save callback with the current documents, editor name, and verification flag.
Reset the saving state after the save operation completes.
Check if there are unsaved changes.
IF unsaved changes exist, prompt the user for confirmation before canceling.
IF user confirms or there are no changes, call the cancel callback.
In create mode, check if any document has non-empty content.
In edit mode, compare current document content with initial content.
Return true if any document has changed from its initial state.
